scope.definition: Operator organs named by Cabinet, plus WGX and the external GitHub/CI/runtime authority plane.
liveChecks: git status and open pull requests for the target repositories
user and system systemd service/timer inventory
Grabowski deployment identity and worktree inventory
cross-repository source/config references excluding documentation-only paths

evidenceRefs.reference: grabowski_status and grabowski_context full; deployed head b69fc0d57a64bbc658deebfe4a707f1551e65c8c
systemctl --user list-units --all --type=service --type=timer plus system unit inventory
bounded cross-repository scan of source, config, workflows, policy, schema and registry paths; docs/tests excluded

summary.highestLeverage: Reduce duplicated status/coordination paths before adding new organs or evidence formats.

organs.authority: Commitments, ordering, dependencies, claims, dispatch envelopes, completion verification and receipts.
duplicateData: queue and task status are restated in Cabinet, Leitstand and narrative reports
ten active timer lanes produce overlapping discovery, curation, verification, review and bridge projections

drivers: many operational organs and timer units

conditions: replace cross-repository claims and deterministic completion receipts
migrate all active dispatch and queue consumers
shutdown.partialShutdown: Individual Ops timers are highly disableable because Bureau Core remains authoritative.

organs.recommendation: Keep Bureau Core. Audit and consolidate timer lanes; prefer one scheduler pipeline with explicit stages over independent periodic organs.

organs.authority: Local and fleet execution, processes, resource leases, durable tasks, workers and runtime effects.
duplicateData: task state is also projected into Bureau and Leitstand
multiple old worktrees and transient task units retain historical execution state

drivers: 21 failed or bad-setting transient task units observed
multiple watchdog and Dauersong timer lanes

conditions: replace typed local/fleet execution and audited mutation paths
shutdown.partialShutdown: Optional autonomous, watchdog and historical transient-unit surfaces can be disabled independently.

organs.recommendation: Keep the operator core. Prioritize lifecycle cleanup and task reconciliation over adding more execution roles or receipts.

organs.authority: No domain truth; produces source-bound repository context, citations, snapshots and freshness identity.
duplicateData: several report, sidecar, bundle and projected context formats describe the same source tree
Cabinet and agent workflows may retain additional copies of generated artifacts

conditions: agents fall back to direct repository reads
citation and freshness guarantees are accepted as weaker
shutdown.partialShutdown: UI and redundant export formats can be removed while keeping query, range and context-pack primitives.

organs.recommendation: Keep the context primitives. Consolidate daily use around manifest, context pack and freshness identity; make full reports diagnostic outputs.

organs.authority: Meaning, evidence framing, readable decisions, map semantics and read-only coherence findings.
duplicateData: two ecosystem maps, graph registries, seed files, claims and narrative blueprints overlap
legacy rooms and active rooms preserve parallel navigation structures
status wording can duplicate source-system projections

conditions: retain decisions and authority map in version control
shutdown.partialShutdown: The active Cabinet service can stop without losing GitHub, Bureau, CI or runtime truth.

organs.recommendation: Keep one semantic authority matrix and one generated map. Freeze new rooms and duplicate projections until existing paths are consolidated.

organs.authority: Action-specific readiness is claimed externally, while its own contract says it is not a canonical source of truth.
observedConsumers: Bureau references to readiness and repo-state assessment
duplicateData: repository status, branch drift and action readiness overlap GitHub, Grabowski and Bureau
two bounded mutators overlap Grabowski Git execution

drivers: read-only observation plus derivation, fetch and mutation classes

conditions: migrate the operator report used by Grabowski
move or retire switch-main and fast-forward-only mutation paths
shutdown.partialShutdown: New feature work can stop immediately while existing reports remain available.

organs.recommendation: Freeze scope. Decide one role: either a small repo-state/readiness library consumed by Grabowski, or retire it after migration.

organs.authority: Display and operator interaction only; source systems remain authoritative.
duplicateData: renders Bureau, Grabowski, Cabinet and runtime status already available at source
can overlap Cabinet maps and Steuerboard reports

conditions: operators accept source-specific CLI and web views
shutdown.partialShutdown: Individual panels and duplicate status feeds can be removed independently.

organs.recommendation: Retain as the single operator display. Do not give it task, readiness or runtime truth authority.

organs.authority: Visual projections, controlled Miro surfaces and publishable renderings.
observedConsumers: human review, teaching and Miro workflows
duplicateData: generic ecosystem maps overlap Cabinet map semantics and Leitstand display
snapshot and view registries duplicate source metadata for rendering

conditions: replace Miro and publication workflows where actually used
shutdown.partialShutdown: Generic ecosystem dashboard ambitions can stop while specialized renderers remain.

organs.recommendation: Keep as a specialist renderer. Consume Cabinet or domain sources; do not maintain a second generic ecosystem canon.

organs.authority: Append-only event history and historical evidence, not task or decision authority.
observedConsumers: declared integrations in Grabowski, Plexer, HausKI, Heimgeist, Heimlern, SemantAH and Leitstand
duplicateData: Git, Bureau receipts and runtime logs already preserve much operational history
unbounded event capture would duplicate source logs without a query consumer

drivers: event contracts, retention and consumer gates
no active local Chronik unit observed

conditions: confirm no remote Chronik service is authoritative
retain source logs and Bureau receipts
shutdown.partialShutdown: Optional producers can remain off until a named consumer and query use case exists.

organs.recommendation: Keep only high-value lifecycle events with named consumers. Do not mirror Git commits, diffs or every Bureau transition.

organs.authority: Proposal-only retrospective learning and policy-adjustment suggestions.
observedConsumers: declared HausKI, Heimgeist, SemantAH, WGX and Leitstand integrations
duplicateData: overlaps Grabowski friction analysis, Cabinet outcomes and Vibe-Lab experiments
policy proposal formats exist without a demonstrated automatic consumption loop

conditions: retain existing reports and fixtures as archived evidence
shutdown.partialShutdown: Expansion can pause without affecting Bureau or Grabowski operation.

organs.recommendation: Pause expansion until one closed loop shows that a proposal changes routing or policy and measurably reduces operator friction.

organs.authority: Experiment evidence and promoted practices, not production operation.
duplicateData: experiments, benchmarks, decisions and schemas can duplicate normal repository proofs and review evidence
many proposed experiment contracts have no named production consumer

conditions: preserve adopted practices and completed experiment evidence
shutdown.partialShutdown: New experiments can be blocked unless they have a sponsor, decision target and expiry date.

organs.recommendation: Retain as a bounded lab, not a permanent work generator. Require consumer, decision question and expiry before starting an experiment.

organs.authority: Standardized fleet checks, profiles and CI motor functions; source repositories and CI remain factual authorities.
observedConsumers: Lenskit and other WGX-enabled repository workflows
duplicateData: guards, smoke and metrics can overlap repository-native CI and Grabowski operations
fleet status projections overlap Leitstand and Bureau observation

drivers: no dedicated active local unit observed

conditions: migrate shared guards and metrics to repository-native or reusable GitHub workflows
shutdown.partialShutdown: Operator-like mutation paths can be excluded while standardized CI checks remain.

organs.recommendation: Keep only genuinely shared fleet checks and templates. Do not duplicate Grabowski execution or Bureau coordination.

organs.authority: Branches, pull requests, reviews, CI results and actual service/runtime state.
duplicateData: all local projections risk becoming stale copies

drivers: source repositories, workflows and deployed services

conditions: replace source control, CI and runtime infrastructure
shutdown.partialShutdown: Only derived caches and projections are optional.

organs.recommendation: Treat as primary reality. Every dashboard and audit must preserve source links and freshness instead of copying authority.

actions.decision: Map overlap, ordering and unique output of the ten active Bureau timers; consolidate only after consumers are identified.
Reconcile failed transient task units and classify the 44 worktrees before archive or cleanup.
Freeze scope and decide whether to extract the operator report/readiness library into Grabowski or retain a sharply bounded standalone component.
Reduce maps, rooms and duplicated status prose to one authority matrix, one registry and one generated view.
Require named consumer, decision target, success metric and expiry for Heimlern and Vibe-Lab work.
Define the small event set worth storing beyond Git, Bureau receipts and source logs.
